+  daemon → `control.SessionsNew(ctx, h.SocketPath, "<label>")` → spawn
+  attach → assert. The ticket's AC says "create session via
+  `pyry sessions new`" — using the in-process `control.SessionsNew` client
+  satisfies the same wire-level contract without a second `exec.Command`,
+  matching every other e2e test in this package (`cap_test.go`,
+  `sessions_list_test.go`, `sessions_rm_test.go`, `idle_test.go`).
+- `internal/e2e/cap_test.go:26-35` — `writeSleepClaude` is **not** used by
+  this harness. The supervised claude here is the e2e test binary running
+  TestHelperProcess in echo mode, not /bin/sleep. The reference is
+  defensive: a developer skimming the e2e package may assume sleep-claude
+  is the standard; for stdio-attach round-trip, the helper-as-claude
+  shape (already in `spawnAttachableDaemon`) is the only one that echoes
+  bytes back.
+- `docs/specs/architecture/154-attach-stdio-mode.md` — the spec for the
+  `pyry attach --stdio` surface this ticket exercises end-to-end.
+  Particularly its "Concurrency model" section, which establishes the
+  invariants this test verifies at the binary boundary: stdin EOF →
+  clean detach → exit 0; bytes pass through unfiltered; goroutine joined
+  before return.
+- `docs/specs/architecture/125-e2e-attach-pty-harness.md` — the precedent
+  this ticket mirrors in shape ("intentionally minimal harness + one
+  proof-of-life test"). Particularly its "Out of scope" framing — the
+  follow-on coverage tickets (#162 no-PTY-fd assertion; 1.3c-2 foreground
+  auto-attach) reuse the harness this ticket lands.
+
+# Context
+
+## Why this slice exists
+
+#154 landed `pyry attach --stdio` for SDK consumers. Unit tests (in
+`internal/control/attach_stdio_client_test.go`) cover the wire protocol
+and the byte-bridging primitive against fake servers; the existing
+`internal/e2e` package has zero coverage of `--stdio` because the only
+attach harness (`attach_pty.go`) allocates a PTY and uses the PTY-mode
+attach client.
+
+There is no test that proves a byte written to a programmatic parent's
+stdout pipe travels: parent → `pyry attach --stdio` stdin pipe → control
+socket → bridge → supervisor PTY → claude → and back through the
+`pyry attach --stdio` stdout pipe → parent's stdin pipe. This is the
+exact byte path Claudian (Obsidian + `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk`)
+will exercise in production: SDK `spawn()` over plain pipes, no PTY
+anywhere on the client side.
+
+This ticket lands the harness alongside its first consumer test — the
+smallest meaningful proof that the no-PTY shape works end-to-end. Two
+follow-on tickets (1.3a-e2e-no-pty: assert no PTY fds open in the
+attach client; 1.3c-2-e2e-*: foreground auto-attach) reuse this same
+harness and are out of scope here, mirroring the #125 / #126 / #127 /
+#128 staging.
+
+## Why the harness lives in `internal/e2e/` not `internal/control/`
+
+`internal/control/attach_stdio_client_test.go` already covers the
+client function with fake servers. The gap is at the **binary**
+boundary: `pyry attach --stdio` as a child process, with real stdin /
+stdout pipes, against a real daemon. That is what `internal/e2e/`
+exists for — it owns the daemon-spawn + child-spawn + capture
+machinery (`ensurePyryBuilt`, `childEnv`, `killSpawned`,
+`spawnAttachableDaemon`, `waitDaemonReady`).
+
+## What "no PTY anywhere" means here
+
+- The harness does **not** call `pty.Open()`. No `creack/pty` import.
+- The attach client's stdin / stdout / stderr are bound to plain
+  `os.Pipe()` ends. The pipes are **not** TTYs — `term.IsTerminal(fd)`
+  returns false for both ends.
+- The attach client runs in `--stdio` mode, which has no
+  `IsTerminal` branch (see `attach_stdio_client.go:26-27` godoc): it
+  forwards bytes via straight `io.Copy` regardless of stdio shape.
+- The supervised claude on the daemon side **does** still run under a
+  PTY — that's owned by the supervisor and unchanged by this ticket.
+  The bridge bridges the supervisor's PTY master to the attach client's
+  socket conn either way; what's PTY-less here is the client → user
+  hop, which is exactly what `--stdio` was built for.
+
+# Design
+
+## Approach
+
+Two new files. No edits to existing files.
+
+The harness reuses `spawnAttachableDaemon` and `waitDaemonReady` from
+`attach_pty.go` to bring up a daemon whose supervised claude is the
+e2e test binary running `TestHelperProcess` in echo mode. It then:
+
+1. Creates a session via `control.SessionsNew(ctx, h.SocketPath, "<label>")`
+   — an in-process wire call, same shape as every other e2e test in the
+   package.
+2. Allocates two `os.Pipe()` pairs: one for the attach client's stdin
+   (test → child), one for stdout (child → test).
+3. Spawns `pyry attach -pyry-socket=<sock> --stdio <id>` with stdin
+   bound to the input pipe's read end and stdout bound to the output
+   pipe's write end. Stderr captured to a `*bytes.Buffer` for failure
+   diagnostics.
+4. Closes the parent's copies of `inputR` and `outputW` after Start so
+   that EOF on `outputR` corresponds to child-stdout-close (not a
+   reference held by the parent), and so that closing `inputW` from
+   the test propagates EOF to the child's stdin.
+5. Returns a `*StdioAttachClient` whose methods write to `inputW` and
+   read from `outputR`.
+
+The supervised helper's echo mode is line-buffered (it emits each `\n`
+-terminated line as one write). The round-trip test writes one
+`<nonce>\n` line and reads until that line appears in the accumulated
+output, swallowing the helper's pre-attach `PYRY_E2E_STARTED pid=…\n`
+banner along the way (same pattern `attach_pty_test.go:170-203` uses).
+
+## End-to-end trajectory
+
+```
+test                         harness                            daemon (pyry, bridge mode)             supervised helper (test bin, echo mode)
+─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+startStdioAttach(t, "label") ─> ensurePyryBuilt
+                                home := newRegistryHome(t)
+                                socket, daemonCmd, …  := spawnAttachableDaemon(t, home)
+                                                                ─> pyry stdin = /dev/null (not a TTY)
+                                                                   bridge mode enabled
+                                                                   bootstrap session created
+                                                                   supervisor.runOnce
+                                                                   pty.Start(testBin -test.run=TestHelperProcess)
+                                                                                                       ─> emits "PYRY_E2E_STARTED pid=<pid>\n"
+                                                                                                          (lost to /dev/null until attach)
+                                waitDaemonReady(socket, …)
+                                ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(... 5s)
+                                id, err := control.SessionsNew(ctx, socket, "<label>")
+                                                                ─> sessions.new wire verb:
+                                                                   spawn second helper for new session
+                                                                                                       ─> emits banner (same)
+
+                                inputR, inputW   := os.Pipe()
+                                outputR, outputW := os.Pipe()
+                                stderrBuf        := &bytes.Buffer{}
+
+                                attachCmd := exec.Command(bin,
+                                  "attach",
+                                  "-pyry-socket="+socket,
+                                  "--stdio", id)
+                                attachCmd.Stdin  = inputR
+                                attachCmd.Stdout = outputW
+                                attachCmd.Stderr = stderrBuf
+                                attachCmd.Env    = childEnv(home)
+                                attachCmd.Start()                ─> attach client:
+                                                                   IsTerminal(0)/IsTerminal(1) == false
+                                                                   AttachStdio(ctx, sock, id, os.Stdin, os.Stdout)
+                                                                   handshake: VerbAttach{SessionID:id, Cols:0, Rows:0}
+                                                                   server: handleAttach → bridge.Attach
+                                _ = inputR.Close()                  (parent drops its dup so EOF flows from inputW alone)
+                                _ = outputW.Close()                 (parent drops its dup so EOF on outputR == child closed stdout)
+
+                                // Detect early-exit handshake failure
+                                go cmd.Wait → close(attachDone)
+                                select { <-attachDone (within 500ms) → t.Fatalf
+                                       ; <-time.After(500ms)         → continue }
+
+                                return *StdioAttachClient{ inputW, outputR, … }
+
+c.Write([]byte("ping-X\n"))  ────────────────────────────────────>  attach reads inputR
+                                                                    io.Copy(conn, in) writes "ping-X\n" to socket
+                                                                                                       <─ helper reads, accumulates until "\n",
+                                                                                                          writes line to stdout
+                                                                    io.Copy(out, conn) writes to outputW       ─>
+c.ReadUntil([]byte("ping-X\n"), 5s)
+                             <───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  outputR.Read drains the line
+                                (and any prior banner bytes)
+
+c.Close() (or t.Cleanup):
+                                inputW.Close()  ─> child stdin EOF ─> AttachStdio in-loop returns ─> conn.Close ─> server bridge detach
+                                                                      (session stays alive — lazy-eviction)
+                                                                      AttachStdio joins output goroutine, returns nil
+                                                                      runAttach prints nothing (--stdio suppresses), exit 0
+                                <-attachDone (within ~2s); else killSpawned(attachCmd)
+                                outputR.Close()
+                                killSpawned(daemonCmd)
+                                os.Remove(socket)
+```
+
+## Package structure
+
+Two new files. No edits to existing files.
+
+### NEW `internal/e2e/attach_stdio.go`
+
+```go
+//go:build e2e
+```
+
+The build tag is `e2e` only — the PTY harness uses `e2e || e2e_install`
+for installer-test reuse, but the stdio harness has no installer-test
+consumer (the installer doesn't ship `pyry attach --stdio` semantics
+beyond the binary itself, and the existing installer tests already
+cover binary presence). Keeping the gate to `e2e` matches the test
+file's gate and avoids compiling dead weight on installer runs.
+
+Approximate shape (~110 LOC excluding comments):
+
+```go
+package e2e
+
+import (
+    "bytes"
+    "context"
+    "fmt"
+    "os"
+    "os/exec"
+    "sync"
+    "testing"
+    "time"
+
+    "github.com/pyrycode/pyrycode/internal/control"
+)
+
+// StdioAttachClient is a programmatic peer for `pyry attach --stdio`,
+// wired via plain os.Pipe() — no PTY, no terminal, no raw mode. Tests
+// drive the supervised session by writing to the client's stdin pipe
+// (Write) and reading from its stdout pipe (ReadUntil / Read). Close
+// closes the stdin pipe, which propagates EOF through the attach
+// client and ends the attach cleanly without destroying the session.
+//
+// Returned by startStdioAttach. Cleanup is registered via t.Cleanup
+// at construction.
+type StdioAttachClient struct {
+    // SessionID is the id of the session this client is attached to,
+    // as returned by control.SessionsNew. Exposed for diagnostics and
+    // for tests that want to drive other CLI verbs against the same
+    // session.
+    SessionID string
+
+    // SocketPath / HomeDir mirror the daemon harness fields.
+    SocketPath string
+    HomeDir    string
+
+    // Stderr captures the attach client's stderr. Empty in steady
+    // state — `--stdio` mode suppresses pyry's own stderr noise — so
+    // any content here is a failure diagnostic.
+    Stderr *bytes.Buffer
+
+    inputW  *os.File   // parent's write end of attach client's stdin
+    outputR *os.File   // parent's read end of attach client's stdout
+
+    daemonCmd  *exec.Cmd
+    daemonDone chan struct{}
+    daemonErr  *bytes.Buffer
+
+    attachCmd  *exec.Cmd
+    attachDone chan struct{}
+
+    cleanupOnce sync.Once
+}
+
+// startStdioAttach brings up a pyry daemon in bridge mode (helper-as-
+// claude in echo mode), creates a fresh session via control.SessionsNew
+// with the given label, and spawns `pyry attach --stdio <id>` whose
+// stdin and stdout are wired to plain os.Pipe()s. Returns a
+// StdioAttachClient the test uses to write/read bytes.
+//
+// label is the human-facing session label passed to sessions.new. Pass
+// "" for unlabeled (the server treats nil and empty-Label identically).
+//
+// Skips the test (t.Skip) if os.Pipe() fails — extremely rare, only
+// observed in heavily sandboxed containers without /dev/null-style fd
+// allocation. Fails the test on any other startup error (daemon spawn,
+// readiness timeout, sessions.new wire error, attach client spawn,
+// attach client early exit).
+func startStdioAttach(t *testing.T, label string) *StdioAttachClient { ... }
+
+// Write writes b to the attach client's stdin pipe. Returns the
+// underlying os.File.Write result; callers typically expect len(b),
+// nil on a healthy attach.
+func (c *StdioAttachClient) Write(b []byte) (int, error) { ... }
+
+// ReadUntil reads from the attach client's stdout pipe in a loop,
+// accumulating bytes, until needle appears in the accumulated buffer
+// or the overall deadline elapses. The accumulated bytes (including
+// any pre-needle banner emitted by the helper) are visible in the
+// returned buffer regardless of outcome.
+//
+// Mirrors readUntilContains in attach_pty_test.go (which os.Pipe ends
+// share the no-SetReadDeadline trait with PTY masters on darwin —
+// the deadline is enforced via a select against a background read
+// goroutine).
+func (c *StdioAttachClient) ReadUntil(needle []byte, total time.Duration) ([]byte, error) { ... }
+
+// Close closes the attach client's stdin pipe (delivering EOF to the
+// child's AttachStdio input loop), waits up to ~2s for the child to
+// exit cleanly, and returns its exit code. Subsequent calls return
+// the same exit code from a cached ProcessState.
+//
+// Idempotent. Also called from t.Cleanup; whichever fires first wins.
+func (c *StdioAttachClient) Close(t *testing.T) int { ... }
+
+// teardown is the sync.Once-wrapped cleanup body. Ordering:
+//   1. inputW.Close()       — sends EOF to child stdin → clean detach
+//   2. wait attachDone (≤ ~2s) → killSpawned(attachCmd) on timeout
+//   3. outputR.Close()      — unblocks any in-flight ReadUntil
+//   4. killSpawned(daemonCmd)
+//   5. os.Remove(socketPath)
+func (c *StdioAttachClient) teardown(t *testing.T) { ... }
+```
+
+### NEW `internal/e2e/attach_stdio_test.go`
+
+```go
+//go:build e2e
+```
+
+Approximate shape (~40 LOC):
+
+```go
+package e2e
+
+import (
+    "bytes"
+    "testing"
+    "time"
+)
+
+// TestE2E_AttachStdio_BytesRoundTrip proves that a byte written into a
+// programmatic parent's stdin-of-`pyry attach --stdio` travels: parent
+// pipe → attach client → control socket → bridge → supervisor PTY →
+// supervised helper → and back through the attach client's stdout into
+// the parent's read pipe. The helper is the e2e test binary running
+// TestHelperProcess in echo mode (line-buffered). The test writes one
+// nonce-tagged line and asserts the same line appears on output within
+// a generous deadline; pre-line banner bytes (the helper's startup
+// "PYRY_E2E_STARTED pid=…\n") are accumulated and ignored, identical
+// to the PTY harness's readUntilContains discipline.
+//
+// This test is the smallest meaningful exercise of the stdio-attach
+// shape end-to-end. Follow-up tickets (1.3a-e2e-no-pty: assert no PTY
+// fd open in the client; 1.3c-2-e2e-*: foreground auto-attach) reuse
+// startStdioAttach for their own scenarios.
+func TestE2E_AttachStdio_BytesRoundTrip(t *testing.T) {
+    c := startStdioAttach(t, "stdio-roundtrip")
+
+    // Trailing \n is required: the helper's echo mode is line-buffered
+    // and only flushes a line when it sees \n.
+    payload := []byte("pyry-stdio-roundtrip-" + tinyNonce() + "\n")
+
+    if _, err := c.Write(payload); err != nil {
+        t.Fatalf("write: %v", err)
+    }
+
+    seen, err := c.ReadUntil(payload, 5*time.Second)
+    if err != nil {
+        t.Fatalf("did not observe payload back: %v\nstderr:\n%s",
+            err, c.Stderr.String())
+    }
+
+    // Defensive: the needle must appear *after* any banner. The
+    // ReadUntil contract already guarantees needle ∈ seen on nil err,
+    // so this is a smoke check that the buffer wasn't returned empty
+    // by an unlikely API regression.
+    if !bytes.Contains(seen, payload) {
+        t.Fatalf("ReadUntil returned without payload in buffer: %q", seen)
+    }
+}
+```
+
+`tinyNonce` already exists in `attach_pty_test.go` (same package, same
+build tag) and is reused as-is.
+
+## Concurrency model
+
+- **Daemon process**: spawned by `spawnAttachableDaemon`, monitored by
+  the existing single-goroutine `cmd.Wait → close(daemonDone)` pattern
+  inside that function. No change.
+
+- **Attach client process**: spawned by the new harness with one
+  `cmd.Wait → close(attachDone)` goroutine. Same shape as the PTY
+  harness's `attach_pty.go:127-131`.
+
+- **Read goroutine inside ReadUntil**: a single short-lived goroutine
+  per outstanding read, identical to `readUntilContains` in
+  `attach_pty_test.go:170-203`. The goroutine reads into a 4096-byte
+  buffer and pushes the result onto a `chan readResult`; the caller
+  selects between the channel and a deadline. No deadline is set on
+  the file (os.Pipe ends on darwin do not support SetReadDeadline,
+  per `attach_pty_test.go:165-168`'s comment).
+
+- **Test goroutines**: none beyond the implicit `cmd.Wait` drainer and
+  the per-call read goroutine. The test writes synchronously, reads
+  with a deadline, asserts.
+
+- **No channels-with-cancel.** The conn-as-rendezvous-point discipline
+  from `AttachStdio` (closing inputW is the wake-up for the attach
+  client's input copy) carries through here: closing inputW from the
+  test process unblocks the child's `io.Copy(conn, in)` and ends the
+  attach.
+
+## Cleanup ordering
+
+Load-bearing — get this wrong and EOF / SIGHUP cascades through the
+wrong end. The harness's `teardown` runs (via `t.Cleanup` and / or an
+explicit `Close`) once, in this order:
+
+1. **`inputW.Close()`** — propagates EOF through the kernel pipe to
+   the attach client's stdin. The client's `AttachStdio` input loop
+   returns nil, the conn closes, the output goroutine joins, and
+   `pyry attach --stdio` exits 0. Clean-detach contract.
+
+2. **Wait on `attachDone` with a short timeout (~2s)**. On clean detach
+   the child exits within milliseconds. If it doesn't, `killSpawned`
+   escalates SIGTERM → grace → SIGKILL (existing helper).
+
+3. **`outputR.Close()`** — releases any in-flight ReadUntil that's
+   blocked on `outputR.Read`. Done after the child has exited so we
+   don't race the child's stdout writes.
+
+4. **`killSpawned(daemonCmd)`** — SIGTERM the daemon. Pyry's signal
+   handler tears down the supervisor, which SIGKILLs the helper child.
+   Wait on `daemonDone`.
+
+5. **`os.Remove(socketPath)`** — defensive; pyry removes the socket on
+   clean shutdown, but a SIGKILL path (step 4 escalation) skips that.
+
+Wrapped in `sync.Once` so an explicit `c.Close(t)` from the test body
+plus the `t.Cleanup` registered at construction don't double-fire.
+
+## Error handling
+
+| Failure | Behaviour |
+|---|---|
+| `os.Pipe()` returns error | `t.Skipf("e2e: os.Pipe unavailable: %v", err)`. AC#3 — same gating shape as #125's `pty.Open` skip. Realistic only in heavily sandboxed containers; mainline CI has plenty of fd headroom. |
+| `spawnAttachableDaemon` failure | `t.Fatalf` (existing helper already does this). |
+| `waitDaemonReady` timeout / early exit | `t.Fatalf` with daemon stderr. |
+| `control.SessionsNew` error | `t.Fatalf` with the wrapped error. Includes wire errors (`ErrorCode`-bearing responses) and dial / decode failures. |
+| `attachCmd.Start()` error | `t.Fatalf` — fork failure is environmental, not a test bug. |
+| Attach client exits within 500ms of start (handshake failure) | `t.Fatalf` with attach client's exit code and the captured `Stderr` buffer (e.g. "attach: no attach provider configured…" if the daemon somehow landed in foreground mode, or a wire-decode error if pyry's protocol shifted). The PTY harness uses the same pattern at `attach_pty.go:137-146`. |
+| `c.Write` error | Surface as the underlying `os.File.Write` error to the caller. The test will `t.Fatalf` on the spot. |
+| `c.ReadUntil` deadline | Return a `fmt.Errorf("timeout after %s; seen %d bytes: %q", total, len(seen), seen)`. The test wraps with the captured Stderr in its `t.Fatalf` for diagnostics. |
+| Cleanup partial failure | `t.Logf` only, never `t.Fatal` from a `t.Cleanup`. Matches `harness.teardown` discipline. A leaked pyry process in the temp HOME has bounded lifetime — the test process exit closes its socket and idle eviction is disabled, but the daemon's signal handler also fires on the parent's death via SIGHUP. |
+
+The clean-detach contract (`inputW.Close()` → attach client exit 0) is
+load-bearing: it's the production shape an SDK consumer will see when
+its parent process closes the spawned child's stdin. A non-zero exit
+on this path is a real bug — `c.Close(t)` returns the exit code so
+follow-up tickets can assert on it.
+
+# Testing strategy
+
+The harness has no dedicated unit tests — it exists to serve
+`TestE2E_AttachStdio_BytesRoundTrip`, and that test is its acceptance.
+Properties exercised:
+
+1. **Daemon comes up in bridge mode with helper-as-claude.** Implicit:
+   if pyry runs in foreground mode or claude is misconfigured, the
+   server's `handleAttach` rejects with "no attach provider configured"
+   and the harness's early-exit detector surfaces it.
+2. **`sessions.new` wire verb works under the helper claude.** Implicit:
+   if `SessionsNew` fails (server can't spawn the new session because
+   the helper rejects its CLI args), the harness fails before reaching
+   attach.
+3. **`pyry attach --stdio` handshake completes against a real daemon.**
+   Implicit: handshake errors surface in the early-exit window with the
+   captured stderr.
+4. **Bytes round-trip through pipes (no PTY) end-to-end.** The proof-
+   of-life test asserts this directly.
+5. **Clean detach via stdin EOF returns exit 0.** Implicit in the
+   teardown path; the harness asserts cleanly via `attachDone` waited
+   in `Close(t)`. A future ticket can assert exit 0 explicitly when
+   the AC calls for it.
+
+What this slice does **not** verify:
+
+- That no PTY fd is open in the attach client (1.3a-e2e-no-pty, #162).
+  That ticket will inspect `/proc/<pid>/fd` (Linux) or `lsof -p <pid>`
+  (macOS) for the attach client and assert no `/dev/pt[my]/*`-style
+  entries.
+- Foreground auto-attach scenarios (1.3c-2-e2e-*).
+- Server-initiated detach (session evicted from under the client).
+- Multi-session attach exclusivity.
+- Binary-safe transport for arbitrary byte sequences (NUL bytes, CR
+  without LF). The unit tests in
+  `internal/control/attach_stdio_client_test.go` already pin this at
+  the wire boundary; e2e adds nothing.
+
+## Skip-on-no-pipe
+
+`AC#3` requires the test to skip cleanly on hosts without spawn
+capability. `os.Pipe()` is the cleanest gate — it exercises kernel fd
+allocation directly. Place the `os.Pipe()` call before
+`spawnAttachableDaemon` so a clean `t.Skip` is faster than spawning
+pyry and tearing it down. In practice this skip never fires on the
+project's CI matrix; it exists as a defense against future changes
+that drop the e2e suite into a more restrictive environment.
+
+```go
+inputR, inputW, err := os.Pipe()
+if err != nil {
+    t.Skipf("e2e: os.Pipe unavailable: %v", err)
+}
+outputR, outputW, err := os.Pipe()
+if err != nil {
+    _ = inputR.Close()
+    _ = inputW.Close()
+    t.Skipf("e2e: os.Pipe unavailable: %v", err)
+}
+```
+
+# What this spec does not solve
+
+- **No reuse of an "attach harness" abstraction across PTY and stdio
+  modes.** The two harnesses share `spawnAttachableDaemon` and
+  `waitDaemonReady` (free functions in `attach_pty.go`); they do not
+  share a `Harness` or `AttachHarness` type because the surface they
+  expose differs in a load-bearing way: the PTY harness exposes a
+  `*os.File` master, the stdio harness exposes Read / Write methods
+  with line-aware semantics. A common interface would force one shape
+  to bear the other's wart and saves no code in either.
+
+- **No new options-struct on `spawnAttachableDaemon`.** The existing
+  function already does exactly what stdio needs (helper-as-claude,
+  echo mode, idle timeout off, resume off). Reuse without parameter
+  growth.
+
+- **No CLI-level driver for `pyry sessions new`.** The AC says "via
+  `pyry sessions new`" colloquially; every other e2e test in the
+  package uses `control.SessionsNew` (the in-process wire client) for
+  the same effect. Spawning a third subprocess for one wire call is
+  pure cost. The wire-level contract is identical.
+

18	// StdioAttachClient is a programmatic peer for `pyry attach --stdio`,
19	// wired via plain os.Pipe() — no PTY, no terminal, no raw mode. Tests
20	// drive the supervised session by writing to the client's stdin pipe
21	// (Write) and reading from its stdout pipe (ReadUntil). Close closes
22	// the stdin pipe, which propagates EOF through the attach client and
23	// ends the attach cleanly without destroying the session.
24	//
25	// Returned by startStdioAttach. Cleanup is registered via t.Cleanup
26	// at construction.
27	type StdioAttachClient struct {
28		// SessionID is the id of the session this client is attached to,
29		// as returned by control.SessionsNew. Exposed for diagnostics and
30		// for tests that want to drive other CLI verbs against the same
31		// session.
32		SessionID string
33	
34		// SocketPath / HomeDir mirror the daemon harness fields.
35		SocketPath string
36		HomeDir    string
37	
38		// Stderr captures the attach client's stderr. Empty in steady
39		// state — `--stdio` mode suppresses pyry's own stderr noise — so
40		// any content here is a failure diagnostic.
41		Stderr *bytes.Buffer
42	
43		inputW  *os.File // parent's write end of attach client's stdin
44		outputR *os.File // parent's read end of attach client's stdout
45	
46		daemonCmd  *exec.Cmd
47		daemonDone chan struct{}
48		daemonErr  *bytes.Buffer
49	
50		attachCmd  *exec.Cmd
51		attachDone chan struct{}
52	
53		cleanupOnce sync.Once
54	}
55	
56	// startStdioAttach brings up a pyry daemon in bridge mode (helper-as-
57	// claude in echo mode), creates a fresh session via control.SessionsNew
58	// with the given label, and spawns `pyry attach --stdio <id>` whose
59	// stdin and stdout are wired to plain os.Pipe()s. Returns a
60	// StdioAttachClient the test uses to write/read bytes.
61	//
62	// label is the human-facing session label passed to sessions.new. Pass
63	// "" for unlabeled (the server treats nil and empty-Label identically).
64	//
65	// Skips the test (t.Skip) if os.Pipe() fails — extremely rare, only
66	// observed in heavily sandboxed containers without /dev/null-style fd
67	// allocation. Fails the test on any other startup error (daemon spawn,
68	// readiness timeout, sessions.new wire error, attach client spawn,
69	// attach client early exit).
70	func startStdioAttach(t *testing.T, label string) *StdioAttachClient {
71		t.Helper()
72	
73		// Probe pipe availability up front. AC#3 — same gating shape as the
74		// PTY harness's pty.Open skip.
75		inputR, inputW, err := os.Pipe()
76		if err != nil {
77			t.Skipf("e2e: os.Pipe unavailable: %v", err)
78		}
79		outputR, outputW, err := os.Pipe()
80		if err != nil {
81			_ = inputR.Close()
82			_ = inputW.Close()
83			t.Skipf("e2e: os.Pipe unavailable: %v", err)
84		}
85	
86		// Short prefix keeps the socket path under macOS's 104-byte sun_path
87		// limit; t.TempDir() embeds the (long) test name and risks overflow.
88		home, err := os.MkdirTemp("", "pyry-as-*")
89		if err != nil {
90			_ = inputR.Close()
91			_ = inputW.Close()
92			_ = outputR.Close()
93			_ = outputW.Close()
94			t.Fatalf("e2e: mkdtemp home: %v", err)
95		}
96		t.Cleanup(func() { _ = os.RemoveAll(home) })
97	
98		socket, daemonCmd, _, daemonErr, daemonDone := spawnAttachableDaemon(t, home)
99	
100		c := &StdioAttachClient{
101			SocketPath: socket,
102			HomeDir:    home,
103			Stderr:     &bytes.Buffer{},
104			inputW:     inputW,
105			outputR:    outputR,
106			daemonCmd:  daemonCmd,
107			daemonDone: daemonDone,
108			daemonErr:  daemonErr,
109		}
110		t.Cleanup(func() { c.teardown(t) })
111	
112		if err := waitDaemonReady(socket, daemonDone, daemonErr); err != nil {
113			_ = inputR.Close()
114			_ = outputW.Close()
115			t.Fatalf("e2e: %v", err)
116		}
117	
118		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second)
119		defer cancel()
120		id, err := control.SessionsNew(ctx, socket, label)
121		if err != nil {
122			_ = inputR.Close()
123			_ = outputW.Close()
124			t.Fatalf("e2e: sessions.new: %v", err)
125		}
126		c.SessionID = id
127	
128		bin := ensurePyryBuilt(t)
129		attachCmd := exec.Command(bin, "attach", "-pyry-socket="+socket, "--stdio", id)
130		attachCmd.Stdin = inputR
131		attachCmd.Stdout = outputW
132		attachCmd.Stderr = c.Stderr
133		attachCmd.Env = childEnv(home)
134	
135		if err := attachCmd.Start(); err != nil {
136			_ = inputR.Close()
137			_ = outputW.Close()
138			t.Fatalf("e2e: pyry attach --stdio start: %v", err)
139		}
140	
141		// The child holds its own dups of inputR and outputW. Close the
142		// parent's copies so EOF on outputR corresponds to child-stdout-close
143		// (not a reference held by the parent), and so closing inputW from
144		// the test propagates EOF to the child's stdin.
145		_ = inputR.Close()
146		_ = outputW.Close()
147	
148		attachDone := make(chan struct{})
149		go func() {
150			_ = attachCmd.Wait()
151			close(attachDone)
152		}()
153		c.attachCmd = attachCmd
154		c.attachDone = attachDone
155	
156		// If the attach client dies in handshake, surface that early instead
157		// of letting the test wait out its read deadline.
158		select {
159		case <-attachDone:
160			exit := -1
161			if attachCmd.ProcessState != nil {
162				exit = attachCmd.ProcessState.ExitCode()
163			}
164			t.Fatalf("e2e: pyry attach --stdio exited before round-trip (exit=%d)\nattach stderr:\n%s\ndaemon stderr:\n%s",
165				exit, c.Stderr.String(), daemonErr.String())
166		case <-time.After(500 * time.Millisecond):
167		}
168	
169		return c
170	}
171	
172	// Write writes b to the attach client's stdin pipe. Returns the
173	// underlying os.File.Write result; callers typically expect len(b),
174	// nil on a healthy attach.
175	func (c *StdioAttachClient) Write(b []byte) (int, error) {
176		return c.inputW.Write(b)
177	}
178	
179	// ReadUntil reads from the attach client's stdout pipe in a loop in a
180	// background goroutine, accumulating bytes, until needle appears in the
181	// accumulated buffer or the overall deadline elapses. The accumulated
182	// bytes (including any pre-needle banner emitted by the helper) are
183	// returned regardless of outcome.
184	//
185	// Mirrors readUntilContains in attach_pty_test.go: os.Pipe ends share
186	// the no-SetReadDeadline trait with PTY masters on darwin, so the
187	// timeout is enforced by the caller rather than per-read.
188	func (c *StdioAttachClient) ReadUntil(needle []byte, total time.Duration) ([]byte, error) {
189		type readResult struct {
190			buf []byte
191			err error
192		}
193		ch := make(chan readResult, 1)
194		var seen bytes.Buffer
195	
196		read := func() {
197			buf := make([]byte, 4096)
198			n, err := c.outputR.Read(buf)
199			ch <- readResult{buf: buf[:n], err: err}
200		}
201	
202		deadline := time.Now().Add(total)
203		go read()
204		for {
205			select {
206			case res := <-ch:
207				if len(res.buf) > 0 {
208					seen.Write(res.buf)
209					if bytes.Contains(seen.Bytes(), needle) {
210						return seen.Bytes(), nil
211					}
212				}
213				if res.err != nil {
214					return seen.Bytes(), fmt.Errorf("read: %w (seen %q)", res.err, seen.Bytes())
215				}
216				go read()
217			case <-time.After(time.Until(deadline)):
218				return seen.Bytes(), fmt.Errorf("timeout after %s; seen %d bytes: %q", total, seen.Len(), seen.Bytes())
219			}
220		}
221	}
222	
223	// Close closes the attach client's stdin pipe (delivering EOF to the
224	// child's AttachStdio input loop), waits up to ~2s for the child to
225	// exit cleanly, and returns its exit code. Subsequent calls return
226	// the same exit code from the cached ProcessState.
227	//
228	// Idempotent. Also called from t.Cleanup; whichever fires first wins.
229	func (c *StdioAttachClient) Close(t *testing.T) int {
230		t.Helper()
231		c.teardown(t)
232		if c.attachCmd != nil && c.attachCmd.ProcessState != nil {
233			return c.attachCmd.ProcessState.ExitCode()
234		}
235		return -1
236	}
237	
238	// teardown is the sync.Once-wrapped cleanup body. Ordering:
239	//  1. inputW.Close()       — sends EOF to child stdin → clean detach
240	//  2. wait attachDone (≤ ~2s) → killSpawned(attachCmd) on timeout
241	//  3. outputR.Close()      — unblocks any in-flight ReadUntil
242	//  4. killSpawned(daemonCmd)
243	//  5. os.Remove(socketPath)
244	func (c *StdioAttachClient) teardown(t *testing.T) {
245		t.Helper()
246		c.cleanupOnce.Do(func() {
247			if c.inputW != nil {
248				_ = c.inputW.Close()
249			}
250			if c.attachCmd != nil && c.attachCmd.Process != nil {
251				select {
252				case <-c.attachDone:
253				case <-time.After(2 * time.Second):
254					killSpawned(t, c.attachCmd, c.attachDone)
255				}
256			}
257			if c.outputR != nil {
258				_ = c.outputR.Close()
259			}
260			if c.daemonCmd != nil && c.daemonCmd.Process != nil {
261				killSpawned(t, c.daemonCmd, c.daemonDone)
262			}
263			_ = os.Remove(c.SocketPath)
264		})
265	}

3	package e2e
4	
5	import (
6		"bytes"
7		"testing"
8		"time"
9	)
10	
11	// TestE2E_AttachStdio_BytesRoundTrip proves that a byte written into a
12	// programmatic parent's stdin-of-`pyry attach --stdio` travels: parent
13	// pipe → attach client → control socket → bridge → supervisor PTY →
14	// supervised helper → and back through the attach client's stdout into
15	// the parent's read pipe. The helper is the e2e test binary running
16	// TestHelperProcess in echo mode (line-buffered). The test writes one
17	// nonce-tagged line and asserts the same line appears on output within
18	// a generous deadline; pre-line banner bytes (the helper's startup
19	// "PYRY_E2E_STARTED pid=…\n") are accumulated and ignored, identical
20	// to the PTY harness's readUntilContains discipline.
21	//
22	// This test is the smallest meaningful exercise of the stdio-attach
23	// shape end-to-end. Follow-up tickets (1.3a-e2e-no-pty: assert no PTY
24	// fd open in the client; 1.3c-2-e2e-*: foreground auto-attach) reuse
25	// startStdioAttach for their own scenarios.
26	func TestE2E_AttachStdio_BytesRoundTrip(t *testing.T) {
27		// Blocked on #167 — `pyry attach --stdio` is rejected by
28		// parseClientFlags before parseAttachArgs runs, so the harness can't
29		// drive the CLI through. Once #167 lands, remove this skip and the
30		// existing harness body should make the test pass unchanged.
31		t.Skip("blocked on #167 — pyry attach --stdio rejected by parseClientFlags")
32	
33		c := startStdioAttach(t, "stdio-roundtrip")
34	
35		// Trailing \n is required: the helper's echo mode is line-buffered
36		// and only flushes a line when it sees \n.
37		payload := []byte("pyry-stdio-roundtrip-" + tinyNonce() + "\n")
38	
39		if _, err := c.Write(payload); err != nil {
40			t.Fatalf("write: %v", err)
41		}
42	
43		seen, err := c.ReadUntil(payload, 5*time.Second)
44		if err != nil {
45			t.Fatalf("did not observe payload back: %v\nstderr:\n%s",
46				err, c.Stderr.String())
47		}
48	
49		// Defensive: the needle must appear *after* any banner. The
50		// ReadUntil contract already guarantees needle ∈ seen on nil err,
51		// so this is a smoke check that the buffer wasn't returned empty
52		// by an unlikely API regression.
53		if !bytes.Contains(seen, payload) {
54			t.Fatalf("ReadUntil returned without payload in buffer: %q", seen)
55		}
56	}

content.content:
{"body":"## Summary

`pyry attach --stdio <id>` fails at the CLI level with `flag provided but not defined: -stdio`. The `--stdio` flag is parsed correctly by `parseAttachArgs` (covered by `TestParseAttachArgs` in `cmd/pyry/args_test.go:145`), but `runAttach` calls `parseClientFlags` first, and `parseClientFlags` uses `flag.ContinueOnError` with only `-pyry-name`/`-pyry-socket` defined — any other flag-shaped arg causes Parse to error out before the args ever reach `parseAttachArgs`.

## Reproduction

```
$ pyry attach --stdio some-id
flag provided but not defined: -stdio
[…]
$ pyry attach -pyry-socket=/tmp/foo --stdio some-id
flag provided but not defined: -stdio
[…]
```

Both invocation orders fail; there is no CLI argument arrangement that lets the documented `pyry attach [-pyry-socket=...] --stdio [<id>]` shape work.

## Expected vs actual

**Expected:** `pyry attach --stdio <id>` dispatches to `control.AttachStdio` (per spec `docs/specs/architecture/154-attach-stdio-mode.md` line 122 and `cmd/pyry/main.go:1084`).

**Actual:** `parseClientFlags` errors on the first unknown flag. `runAttach` returns the error to `main`, which prints `pyry: flag provided but not defined: -stdio` and exits non-zero.

## Where the bug lives

- `cmd/pyry/main.go:370-378` — `parseClientFlags` calls `fs.Parse(args)` with only `-pyry-name`/`-pyry-socket` registered; any sub-verb-specific flag like `--stdio` is rejected.
- `cmd/pyry/main.go:486-497` — `runAttach` calls `parseClientFlags` first, then `parseAttachArgs`. The unit test `TestParseAttachArgs` exercises only the second-stage parser, so the breakage is invisible to current unit tests.

## Why it wasn't caught

Existing unit tests (`TestParseAttachArgs`) call `parseAttachArgs` directly. There is no test that drives the full `runAttach` arg path or invokes the `pyry` binary with `--stdio`. The e2e harness in #161 (which lands alongside this report) is the first test to exercise the CLI path.

## Reference

- Surfaced by the e2e test in #161 (`TestE2E_AttachStdio_BytesRoundTrip` in `internal/e2e/attach_stdio_test.go`). That test is t.Skip'd until this bug is resolved; once fixed, removing the skip should make it pass.","state":"OPEN","title":"bug: `pyry attach --stdio` rejected by parseClientFlags before parseAttachArgs runs"}

1	//go:build e2e || e2e_install
2	
3	package e2e
4	
5	import (
6		"bytes"
7		"fmt"
8		"net"
9		"os"
10		"os/exec"
11		"path/filepath"
12		"sync"
13		"syscall"

15		"time"
16	
17		"github.com/creack/pty"
18		"golang.org/x/term"
19	)
20	
21	// AttachHarness owns one running pyry daemon configured for interactive
22	// attach (bridge mode, the e2e test binary as claude in echo mode), the
23	// master side of a creack/pty pair, and the running `pyry attach`
24	// subprocess whose stdio is bound to the slave.
25	//
26	// Returned by StartAttach; cleanup is registered via t.Cleanup. Tests
27	// interact with Master to write input bytes and read output bytes back.
28	type AttachHarness struct {
29		// Master is the PTY master. Tests write input bytes here and read
30		// output bytes back. Closed by cleanup.
31		Master *os.File
32	
33		// SocketPath is the daemon's control socket. Exposed so follow-up
34		// tickets can drive additional verbs against the same daemon
35		// (e.g. a second `pyry attach` to assert ErrBridgeBusy).
36		SocketPath string
37	
38		// HomeDir is the daemon's $HOME (a fresh t.TempDir).
39		HomeDir string
40	
41		daemonCmd  *exec.Cmd
42		daemonDone chan struct{}
43		daemonOut  *bytes.Buffer
44		daemonErr  *bytes.Buffer
45	
46		attachCmd  *exec.Cmd
47		attachDone chan struct{}
48	
49		slave    *os.File
50		origTerm *term.State
51	
52		cleanupOnce sync.Once
53	}
54	
55	// StartAttach spawns a pyry daemon in bridge mode whose supervised claude
56	// is the e2e test binary running TestHelperProcess in echo mode, opens a
57	// creack/pty pair, and spawns `pyry attach` with the slave on stdio. The
58	// returned harness exposes Master for the test to write/read.
59	//
60	// sessionID selects the session to attach to. Empty means "the bootstrap
61	// session", which is the only session this harness creates.
62	//
63	// Skips the test (t.Skip) if pty.Open fails — some hosts (sandboxed CI,
64	// minimal containers) lack a usable /dev/ptmx. Fails the test on any
65	// other startup error.
66	func StartAttach(t *testing.T, sessionID string) *AttachHarness {
67		t.Helper()
68	
69		// Probe PTY availability up front. A clean t.Skip is faster than
70		// spawning pyry, racing readiness, then tearing down. AC#5.
71		master, slave, err := pty.Open()
72		if err != nil {
73			t.Skipf("e2e: pty.Open unavailable: %v", err)
74		}
75	
76		// Snapshot the parent test process's stdin terminal state defensively.
77		// No code path in this harness should modify it, but AC#4 calls for a
78		// restore-on-cleanup guarantee.
79		var origTerm *term.State
80		if term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdin.Fd())) {
81			st, err := term.GetState(int(os.Stdin.Fd()))
82			if err == nil {
83				origTerm = st
84			}
85		}
86	
87		// os.MkdirTemp with a short prefix keeps the socket path under macOS's
88		// 104-byte sun_path limit; t.TempDir() embeds the (long) test name and
89		// overflows for callers like TestE2E_Attach_SurvivesClaudeRestart.
90		home, err := os.MkdirTemp("", "pyry-at-*")
91		if err != nil {
92			t.Fatalf("e2e: mkdtemp home: %v", err)
93		}
94		t.Cleanup(func() { _ = os.RemoveAll(home) })
95		socket, daemonCmd, daemonOut, daemonErr, daemonDone := spawnAttachableDaemon(t, home)
96	
97		a := &AttachHarness{
98			Master:     master,
99			SocketPath: socket,
100			HomeDir:    home,
101			daemonCmd:  daemonCmd,
102			daemonDone: daemonDone,
103			daemonOut:  daemonOut,
104			daemonErr:  daemonErr,
105			slave:      slave,
106			origTerm:   origTerm,
107		}
108		t.Cleanup(func() { a.teardown(t) })
109	
110		if err := waitDaemonReady(socket, daemonDone, daemonErr); err != nil {
111			t.Fatalf("e2e: %v", err)
112		}
113	
114		bin := ensurePyryBuilt(t)
115		attachCmd := exec.Command(bin, "attach", "-pyry-socket="+socket)
116		attachCmd.Stdin = slave
117		attachCmd.Stdout = slave
118		attachCmd.Stderr = slave
119		// Make the slave the controlling terminal of the attach client so
120		// IsTerminal(stdin) returns true and term.MakeRaw kills ECHO/ICANON.
121		attachCmd.SysProcAttr = &syscall.SysProcAttr{Setsid: true, Setctty: true}
122		attachCmd.Env = childEnv(home)
123	
124		if err := attachCmd.Start(); err != nil {
125			t.Fatalf("e2e: pyry attach start: %v", err)
126		}
127		attachDone := make(chan struct{})
128		go func() {
129			_ = attachCmd.Wait()
130			close(attachDone)
131		}()
132		a.attachCmd = attachCmd
133		a.attachDone = attachDone
134	
135		// If the attach client dies in handshake, surface that early instead
136		// of letting the test wait out its read deadline.
137		select {
138		case <-attachDone:
139			exit := -1
140			if attachCmd.ProcessState != nil {
141				exit = attachCmd.ProcessState.ExitCode()
142			}
143			t.Fatalf("e2e: pyry attach exited before round-trip (exit=%d)\ndaemon stderr:\n%s",
144				exit, daemonErr.String())
145		case <-time.After(500 * time.Millisecond):
146		}
147	
148		return a
149	}
150	
151	// spawnAttachableDaemon mirrors harness.spawn but with a custom claude
152	// (the e2e test binary running TestHelperProcess), helper-env injection
153	// (GO_TEST_HELPER_PROCESS=1, GO_TEST_HELPER_MODE=echo), and no sleep
154	// sentinel. The daemon's stdin is left at its default (/dev/null) so
155	// IsTerminal returns false and pyry runs in bridge mode.
156	func spawnAttachableDaemon(t *testing.T, home string) (string, *exec.Cmd, *bytes.Buffer, *bytes.Buffer, chan struct{}) {
157		t.Helper()
158		bin := ensurePyryBuilt(t)
159		socket := filepath.Join(home, "pyry.sock")
160	
161		stdout := &bytes.Buffer{}
162		stderr := &bytes.Buffer{}
163	
164		args := []string{
165			"-pyry-socket=" + socket,
166			"-pyry-name=test",
167			"-pyry-claude=" + os.Args[0],
168			"-pyry-idle-timeout=0",
169			// Default ResumeLast prepends --continue on respawn; the test
170			// helper is a Go test binary that doesn't recognize that flag.
171			"-pyry-resume=false",
172			"--",
173			"-test.run=TestHelperProcess",
174		}
175		cmd := exec.Command(bin, args...)
176		cmd.Stdout = stdout
177		cmd.Stderr = stderr
178		// supervisor.runOnce does cmd.Env = append(os.Environ(), helperEnv...),
179		// so env set on the daemon flows through to the supervised helper.
180		cmd.Env = append(childEnv(home),
181			"GO_TEST_HELPER_PROCESS=1",
182			"GO_TEST_HELPER_MODE=echo",
183		)
184	
185		if err := cmd.Start(); err != nil {
186			t.Fatalf("e2e: pyry attachable daemon start: %v", err)
187		}
188	
189		doneCh := make(chan struct{})
190		go func() {
191			_ = cmd.Wait()
192			close(doneCh)
193		}()
194	
195		return socket, cmd, stdout, stderr, doneCh
196	}
197	
198	// waitDaemonReady polls the socket like Harness.waitForReady. Short-
199	// circuits if the daemon exits before ready (e.g. flag parse error).
200	func waitDaemonReady(socket string, doneCh chan struct{}, errBuf *bytes.Buffer) error {
201		deadline := time.Now().Add(readyDeadline)
202		for time.Now().Before(deadline) {
203			if _, err := os.Stat(socket); err == nil {
204				c, err := net.Dial("unix", socket)
205				if err == nil {
206					_ = c.Close()
207					return nil
208				}
209			}
210			select {
211			case <-doneCh:
212				return fmt.Errorf("pyry exited before ready: %s", errBuf.String())
213			case <-time.After(readyPollGap):
214			}
215		}
216		return fmt.Errorf("pyry not ready within %s", readyDeadline)
217	}
218	
219	// WaitDetach blocks until the attach client process exits or timeout
220	// elapses, then returns its exit code. Fails the test on timeout — a
221	// clean detach should be near-instant; a generous deadline here is a
222	// safety net, not a steady-state expectation.
223	//
224	// Safe to call after writing the detach sequence to Master. Subsequent
225	// calls return the same exit code (the attachDone channel stays closed
226	// once Wait() returns; ProcessState is set).
227	func (a *AttachHarness) WaitDetach(t *testing.T, timeout time.Duration) int {
228		t.Helper()
229		select {
230		case <-a.attachDone:
231			if a.attachCmd.ProcessState == nil {
232				t.Fatalf("attach process state nil after Wait")
233			}
234			return a.attachCmd.ProcessState.ExitCode()
235		case <-time.After(timeout):
236			t.Fatalf("attach client did not exit within %s after detach", timeout)
237			return -1 // unreachable
238		}
239	}
240	
241	// Run invokes the cached pyry binary against this harness's daemon
242	// socket with HOME=a.HomeDir and the verb's args appended. Mirrors
243	// Harness.Run — same auto-injection of -pyry-socket=, same RunResult
244	// shape, same timeout. Used by tests that need to drive a CLI verb
245	// against the same daemon the attach client is bound to.
246	func (a *AttachHarness) Run(t *testing.T, verb string, args ...string) RunResult {
247		t.Helper()
248		return runVerb(t, a.SocketPath, a.HomeDir, verb, args...)
249	}
250	
251	// teardown closes master and slave (delivering SIGHUP/EOF to the attach
252	// client), then SIGTERM-grace-SIGKILLs both the attach client and the
253	// daemon, then removes the socket and restores the parent's terminal
254	// state. Idempotent via sync.Once.
255	func (a *AttachHarness) teardown(t *testing.T) {
256		a.cleanupOnce.Do(func() {
257			if a.Master != nil {
258				_ = a.Master.Close()
259			}
260			if a.slave != nil {
261				_ = a.slave.Close()
262			}
263			if a.attachCmd != nil && a.attachCmd.Process != nil {
264				killSpawned(t, a.attachCmd, a.attachDone)
265			}
266			if a.daemonCmd != nil && a.daemonCmd.Process != nil {
267				killSpawned(t, a.daemonCmd, a.daemonDone)
268			}
269			_ = os.Remove(a.SocketPath)
270			if a.origTerm != nil {
271				_ = term.Restore(int(os.Stdin.Fd()), a.origTerm)
272			}
273		})
274	}

content.content:
{"body":"## What

Lands the e2e test harness for `pyry attach --stdio` (#161) plus its proof-of-life round-trip test:

- `internal/e2e/attach_stdio.go` — `startStdioAttach(t, label)` spawns a pyry daemon in bridge mode (helper-as-claude in echo mode), creates a session via `control.SessionsNew`, and runs `pyry attach --stdio <id>` with stdin/stdout wired to plain `os.Pipe()`s — no PTY anywhere on the client side. Returns a `*StdioAttachClient` with `Write`, `ReadUntil`, and `Close` methods. Reuses `spawnAttachableDaemon` and `waitDaemonReady` from `attach_pty.go`. Cleanup ordering follows the architecture spec: `inputW.Close()` → wait `attachDone` → `outputR.Close()` → kill daemon → remove socket, all under `sync.Once`.
- `internal/e2e/attach_stdio_test.go` — `TestE2E_AttachStdio_BytesRoundTrip` writes one nonce-tagged line and asserts the same line appears on output within 5s.

## Issue

Closes #161 (harness lands; the proof-of-life test is committed but t.Skip'd — see below).

## Bug surfaced — out-of-scope, filed as #167

`pyry attach --stdio <id>` is rejected by `parseClientFlags` with `flag provided but not defined: -stdio` **before** `parseAttachArgs` ever sees the flag. The unit tests in `cmd/pyry/args_test.go` exercise `parseAttachArgs` directly, so the breakage was invisible. This harness is the first test to drive the actual CLI path.

Per scope discipline (developer agent CLAUDE.md): bug found out of scope → file separate ticket, do not fix in-place. Filed as **#167** with reproduction + file/line references. The round-trip test is t.Skip'd referencing #167 — once that bug lands, removing the skip should make the test pass against the harness body unchanged.

## Testing

- `go test -race ./...` — clean
- `go test -tags e2e -race ./internal/e2e/...` — clean (the new test cleanly skips with the #167 reference)
- `go vet -tags e2e ./...` — clean

## Architecture compliance

- Two new files only; no edits to existing files (per arch doc "Approach").
- Build tag is `e2e` only (not `e2e || e2e_install`) — matches arch doc rationale: no installer-test consumer.
- Reuses `spawnAttachableDaemon` / `waitDaemonReady` / `ensurePyryBuilt` / `childEnv` / `killSpawned` verbatim — no parameter growth.
- Session creation via `control.SessionsNew` (in-process wire client), matching every other e2e test in the package; no third subprocess for a single wire call.
- Skip-on-no-pipe gate (`os.Pipe()` failure → `t.Skip`) precedes daemon spawn, mirroring `pty.Open` skip in the PTY harness.
- Read-with-deadline pattern mirrors `readUntilContains` in `attach_pty_test.go` (caller-enforced timeout, no `SetReadDeadline` on `os.Pipe` ends).

{"body":"## What

Lands the e2e test harness for `pyry attach --stdio` (#161) plus its proof-of-life round-trip test:

- `internal/e2e/attach_stdio.go` — `startStdioAttach(t, label)` spawns a pyry daemon in bridge mode (helper-as-claude in echo mode), creates a session via `control.SessionsNew`, and runs `pyry attach --stdio <id>` with stdin/stdout wired to plain `os.Pipe()`s — no PTY anywhere on the client side. Returns a `*StdioAttachClient` with `Write`, `ReadUntil`, and `Close` methods. Reuses `spawnAttachableDaemon` and `waitDaemonReady` from `attach_pty.go`. Cleanup ordering follows the architecture spec: `inputW.Close()` → wait `attachDone` → `outputR.Close()` → kill daemon → remove socket, all under `sync.Once`.
- `internal/e2e/attach_stdio_test.go` — `TestE2E_AttachStdio_BytesRoundTrip` writes one nonce-tagged line and asserts the same line appears on output within 5s.

## Issue

Closes #161 (harness lands; the proof-of-life test is committed but t.Skip'd — see below).

## Bug surfaced — out-of-scope, filed as #167

`pyry attach --stdio <id>` is rejected by `parseClientFlags` with `flag provided but not defined: -stdio` **before** `parseAttachArgs` ever sees the flag. The unit tests in `cmd/pyry/args_test.go` exercise `parseAttachArgs` directly, so the breakage was invisible. This harness is the first test to drive the actual CLI path.

Per scope discipline (developer agent CLAUDE.md): bug found out of scope → file separate ticket, do not fix in-place. Filed as **#167** with reproduction + file/line references. The round-trip test is t.Skip'd referencing #167 — once that bug lands, removing the skip should make the test pass against the harness body unchanged.

## Testing

- `go test -race ./...` — clean
- `go test -tags e2e -race ./internal/e2e/...` — clean (the new test cleanly skips with the #167 reference)
- `go vet -tags e2e ./...` — clean

## Architecture compliance

- Two new files only; no edits to existing files (per arch doc "Approach").
- Build tag is `e2e` only (not `e2e || e2e_install`) — matches arch doc rationale: no installer-test consumer.
- Reuses `spawnAttachableDaemon` / `waitDaemonReady` / `ensurePyryBuilt` / `childEnv` / `killSpawned` verbatim — no parameter growth.
- Session creation via `control.SessionsNew` (in-process wire client), matching every other e2e test in the package; no third subprocess for a single wire call.
- Skip-on-no-pipe gate (`os.Pipe()` failure → `t.Skip`) precedes daemon spawn, mirroring `pty.Open` skip in the PTY harness.
- Read-with-deadline pattern mirrors `readUntilContains` in `attach_pty_test.go` (caller-enforced timeout, no `SetReadDeadline` on `os.Pipe` ends).
